fields.name: email
fields.claim: Reserved by published standard; values in this range cannot correspond to a real person or system.

fields.name: name
fields.citation: No standard reserves names; structurally-fake token convention
fields.claim: Structurally synthetic token; not derived from any real record. This field type is not reserved by any standard, so realism is deliberately avoided.

fields.name: phone
fields.claim: Reserved by the issuing authority and never assigned, so no real holder has one. Non-real by administrative policy rather than by protocol.

fields.name: ssn
fields.citation: SSA SSN randomization (effective 2011-06-25): never-assigned area numbers 000 / 666 / 900-999, plus group 00 and serial 0000 (ssa.gov/employer/randomization.html)
fields.claim: Reserved by the issuing authority and never assigned, so no real holder has one. Non-real by administrative policy rather than by protocol.

fields.name: card
fields.citation: Payment-processor / sandbox test PANs (e.g. Stripe testing docs); pass Luhn, authorize nowhere
fields.claim: Designated test value that passes validation; non-real by processor/sandbox designation, not by construction. Valid-looking, but reserved for testing.

fields.name: ip
fields.claim: Reserved by published standard; values in this range cannot correspond to a real person or system.

fields.name: address
fields.citation: No standard reserves addresses; structurally-fake 'Example' convention
fields.claim: Structurally synthetic token; not derived from any real record. This field type is not reserved by any standard, so realism is deliberately avoided.

attestation: This run record attests that the named dataset was generated by SafeSeed from standards-reserved ranges, with no real input data. It is a tamper-evident record, not a cryptographic proof that the file contains no personal data. It attests the generator and binds to this file's content hash; it says nothing about edits made after generation. "Not derived from production data" is not the same claim as "not personal data." Re-verify against the actual artifact in your pipeline (GDPR Art. 25/32 control for non-production environments; not a scope-out from privacy law).
